commands.repair_reason: Contributor and maintainer action is needed because the PR needs a code fix plus redacted current-head Slack proof and an explicit upgrade/docs decision before merge.; Needs attention: The patch commits an internal proof note with operational Slack identifiers, workspace links, local credential-source paths, and secret-source descriptions.; Review finding: [P1] Make the live progress-card path pass before merge — .h... commands.review_summary:
The PR changes Slack progress mode to use native Slack plan/task stream chunks, adds progress identity handling and tests, and adds a Slack trajectory proof helper plus an internal `.hermes` plan note.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review blockers: the PR's own current-head `.hermes` note records a Slack E2E where tool calls ran while the Slack reply had no plan/task blocks until finalization, and source inspection shows failed runs can still finalize in-progress tasks as complete. I did not run live Slack in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Contributor and maintainer action is needed because the PR needs a code fix plus redacted current-head Slack proof and an explicit upgrade/docs decision before merge.

target.title: Render Slack progress drafts as plan cards
target.body:
## Summary
- Preserve Slack native plan/task-card rendering for progress mode instead of falling back to legacy section/fields blocks.
- Stream native Slack `plan_update` / `task_update` chunks for progress events, including single-tool and concurrent progress updates.
- Use the newest meaningful progress step as the native plan title when no explicit label is configured, while preserving explicit configured labels.
- Finalize in-flight native progress tasks so completed replies do not leave stale `Running` cards.

## Verification
- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/slack/src/progress-blocks.test.ts extensions/slack/src/streaming.test.ts extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/dispatch.streaming.test.ts extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/dispatch.preview-fallback.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.extensions.json --pretty false`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --pretty false`

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: Slack native progress/task-card rendering remains on the native plan path, source-run streams native Slack progress while work is executing, and finalization clears running task state.
Real environment tested: local source-run OpenClaw gateway from this PR branch, Slack workspace `4ok`, channel `C0ASNARACMT`, infra-engineer-originated message mentioning prod Jules; gateway config used `openai-codex/gpt-5.5` through the source-run Codex auth store.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: restarted the local source-run gateway from `/home/simon/Workspace/openclaw-slack-plan-progress-drafts`, verified `/healthz`, then posted an infra-engineer Slack message with marker `native-progress-complete-e2e-20260516-154928` asking Jules to run exactly `python3 -c "import time; [print('progress-step-%d' % i, flush=True) or time.sleep(3) for i in range(1,6)]; print('FINAL_MARKER native-progress-complete-e2e-20260516-154928')"`; then polled `conversations.replies`, source-run session/trajectory files, source-run gateway log, and `/healthz` after completion.
Evidence after fix: copied live output from the source-run Slack Web API/session-log proof:
```text
parent_ts=1778946569.986069
reply_ts=1778946586.604129
marker=FINAL_MARKER native-progress-complete-e2e-20260516-154928
slack_block_type=plan
task_card={"type":"task_card","task_id":"bash_1","title":"Bash — Bash","status":"complete"}
final_seen=true
plan_message_count=1
stale_running_tasks=[]
false_error_tasks=[]
healthz_before={"ok":true,"status":"live"}
healthz_after={"ok":true,"status":"live"}
```
Slack replies for parent ts `1778946569.986069` included one native Slack `plan` block in Jules' reply ts `1778946586.604129`; the plan contained the native task card above; the final reply text contained `FINAL_MARKER native-progress-complete-e2e-20260516-154928`.
Observed result after fix: source-run logs showed Slack inbound for the marker plus `slack-stream` start/appends/stop in channel `C0ASNARACMT` thread `1778946569.986069`; the source-run trajectory ended `status: success` with provider/model `codex/gpt-5.5` / `openai-codex-responses`; no duplicate native progress plan messages were observed, no stale `running`/`in_progress` task remained after completion, and gateway health was `{"ok":true,"status":"live"}` before and after the E2E.
What was not tested: no screenshot/video capture of the transient Slack UI; proof is Slack Web API block state plus source-run gateway logs, session trajectory, and health checks.
target.author: simonvanlaak
